title: Create the Gapminder dataset
text:
Code
# Import necessary libraries
import pandas as pd
from gapminder import gapminder

# Rename 'lifeExp' to 'life_expectancy' and 'gdpPercap' to 'gdp_per_capita'
gapminder = gapminder.rename(columns={'lifeExp': 'life_expectancy',
                                      'pop': 'population_millions', 
                                      'gdpPercap': 'gdp_per_capita'})

# Convert population to millions
gapminder['population_millions'] = gapminder['population_millions'] / 1_000_000

# Create a new pandas DataFrame from the modified gapminder data
gapminder_df = pd.DataFrame(gapminder)

# Save the DataFrame as a CSV file
gapminder_df.to_csv('gapminder.csv', index=False)

# Print the first 3 rows of the DataFrame
print(gapminder_df.head(3))


       country continent  year  life_expectancy  population_millions  \

2  Afghanistan      Asia  1962           31.997            10.267083

title: Life Expectancy and GDP per Capita
text:
Introduction: This page explores how wealth (GDP) relates to how long people live.


Code
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px

df = pd.read_csv('gapminder.csv')
fig = px.scatter(df, x="gdp_per_capita", y="life_expectancy", 
                 size="population_millions", color="continent", 
                 hover_name="country", log_x=True, 
                 title="Life Expectancy vs GDP")
fig.show()


                                                


Conclusion: Positive correlation between life expectancy and GDP in all continents, though with different intercepts and slopes.

title: Global Wealth Map
text:
Interactive map: shows the GDP per Capita across the globe for the year 2007:


Code
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px

df = pd.read_csv('gapminder.csv')
df_2007 = df[df['year'] == 2007]

fig = px.choropleth(df_2007, 
                    locations="country", 
                    locationmode="country names", 
                    color="gdp_per_capita", 
                    hover_name="country", 
                    projection="natural earth", 
                    title="GDP per Capita (2007)", 
                    color_continuous_scale=px.colors.sequential.Plasma)
fig.show()

title: Country Analysis: Changes Over Time
text:
This page examines how Life Expectancy, GDP per Capita, and Population have changed over time for Botswana.


Code
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px

# 1. Load the data
df = pd.read_csv('gapminder.csv')

# 2. Filter for Botswana
target_country = 'Botswana'
country_df = df[df['country'] == target_country]

# 3. a multi-line chart or a specific trend chart
# plot Life Expectancy over the years
fig = px.line(country_df, 
              x="year", 
              y="life_expectancy", 
              title=f"Life Expectancy Trends in {target_country}",
              markers=True,
              labels={"year": "Year", "life_expectancy": "Life Expectancy (Years)"})

fig.show()

#GDP change
fig_gdp = px.bar(country_df, 
                 x="year", 
                 y="gdp_per_capita", 
                 title=f"GDP per Capita in {target_country} Over Time",
                 labels={"gdp_per_capita": "GDP per Capita", "year": "Year"})
fig_gdp.show()

#population change
fig_population = px.bar(country_df, 
                 x="year", 
                 y="population_millions", 
                 title=f"Population in {target_country} Over Time",
                 labels={"population": "Population", "year": "Year"})
fig_population.show()







                                                


(a) Trends in Life Expectancy, GDP, and Population






                                                


(b)






                                                


(c)





Figure 1

title: Gapminder Data Analysis
text:
The Gapminder dataset tracks health and wealth indicators for 142 countries over five decades （from 1952 to 2007）. It records values for life expectancy, GDP per capita, and population in every five years.

Quick Links:

Life Expectancy and GDP per Capita
Life Expectancy and Population
Country Analysis

title: Life Expectancy and Population
text:
The relationship between the size of a country’s population and the average life expectancy of its citizens.
Research Question: We want to see if larger countries tend to have higher or lower life expectancy compared to smaller nations.


Code
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px

df = pd.read_csv('gapminder.csv')

fig = px.scatter(df, 
                 x="population_millions", 
                 y="life_expectancy",
                 color="continent",
                 hover_name="country",
                 title="Relationship Between Population and Life Expectancy",
                 labels={
                     "population_millions": "Population (Millions)",
                     "life_expectancy": "Life Expectancy (Years)"
                 })

fig.update_layout(xaxis_type="log")
fig.show()




                                                


Figure 1: Life Expectancy vs. Population (in Millions)




A slight positive correlation between population and life expectancy in all continents.
